title: Stair Baluster Play Vanity
summary: A kid-sized vanity with turned stair-baluster legs, a working slide-out drawer, and a corbel-supported mirror.
description:
Turned stair balusters do the same job here that purchased turned table legs do on a full-size piece — they let a beginner get a genuinely furniture-shop turned-leg look with zero lathe work. Buy the balusters before you build the base, since the base opening has to be sized to actually fit them; the plan's dimensions are a starting point, and you should adjust if your balusters come out a different diameter.

The drawer is the one place precision actually matters: build it to fit your specific slides and opening, not a generic dimension, and expect to trim for an even 1/8" gap around the drawer face once it's installed. The mirror-support corbels may need to be mounted at an angle rather than square, especially with a taller mirror — dry-fit the mirror before committing any screws.

materials.name: Stair balusters, turned
materials.note: Buy these first — the base opening is sized to fit them, and baluster diameter varies by style.

materials.name: Small corbels
materials.note: May need mounting at an angle for a taller mirror — dry-fit before attaching.

materials.name: Edge moulding, 3/4" thick
materials.note: Optional — trims out the plywood top edge.

steps.title: Build the base
steps.body: Assemble the base frame from the 1x4 rails. Before you fasten anything permanently, dry-fit your actual balusters into the leg openings — if the plan's dimensions don't match your balusters' diameter, adjust the opening now rather than fighting it later.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top to the base. Finish nails and glue driven from the top is the simplest approach; 3/4" pocket holes from the underside also work well.

steps.title: Install the legs
steps.body: Set the baluster legs inside the base frame and nail or screw them to the base.

steps.title: Build the drawer to fit your slides
steps.body: Build the drawer box to fit both your slides and the base opening — as a rule of thumb this is about 1" less than the opening's overall width (1/2" clearance per side is standard for most slides), but confirm against your actual slide hardware before cutting.

steps.title: Install the drawer slides
steps.body: Mount the drawer slides to the base and drawer box per the hardware's instructions.

steps.title: Trim the top edge (optional)
steps.body: Trim out the plywood top edge with moulding for a more finished look — this also hides the plywood's raw edge.

steps.title: Attach the drawer face
steps.body: Attach the drawer face to the drawer box, leaving an even 1/8" gap on each side against the opening.

steps.title: Mount the mirror and corbels
steps.body: Support the mirror as needed for its size and weight — the corbels may need to sit at an angle rather than square, especially with a taller mirror. Dry-fit everything before committing any screws.

images.alt: Kid-sized play vanity with turned stair-baluster legs, a slide-out drawer, and a mirror
